cell_problem.proof: for every periodic P1 corrector phi, the means of A+D phi and of every second and third minor equal the corresponding affine minors; the displayed density is convex in the complete minor vector, so Jensen proves phi=0 is a global minimizer on every conforming tetrahedral mesh

checks.name: the simplicial coboundary squares to zero and the shifted cup satisfies exact Leibniz and associativity
antisymmetric exact cups reproduce the affine two-minor and the epsilon third cup reproduces the vertex determinant
the one- and two-cochain Whitney Hodge matrices are strictly positive and rotation covariant
Whitney reconstruction and Hodge energies are exact for affine one- and two-forms
the radially dressed third cup cochain agrees with normalized-affine regular-value degree on all meshes
periodic P1 gradients and two-minors retain their affine means on every six- and five-tet mesh
the finite-R cell minimum is the same rotationally invariant closed formula on every mesh

checks.detail: classical graph-norm density remains separate and explicitly false
checks.name: compatible minors close the liminf and topological-current concentration defect for the relaxed fixed-degree action
all compatible-action representatives are unanchored stationary admissible B=1 fields
the smaller-a/larger-L translation-and-triangulation quotient thresholds are evaluated without premature promotion
the AP-E11 source and triangulation helper are checksummed

cochain_algebra.verdict: the shifted front/back cup is an exact differential graded product; target antisymmetrization recovers the affine exterior minors

degree_cochain.admissibility_lower_bound: if every pair in a tetrahedron has dot product at least epsilon, then |sum lambda_i n_i|^2 >= epsilon+(1-epsilon) sum lambda_i^2 >= (1+3 epsilon)/4

field.profile: compact C1 hedgehog with exact vacuum boundary

degree_cochain.theorem: Omega_h(T)=(orientation(T)/(2 pi^2)) (epsilon n cup dn cup dn cup dn)(T) integral_Delta |sum lambda_i n_i|^(-4) d lambda; its sum is the pullback integral of the normalized-affine map and hence its degree

hodge_star.verdict: the consistent Whitney stars are positive Gram matrices, commute with rigid rotations, and exactly reconstruct constant one-, two-, and three-forms

cases.declared_action: compatible P1 Dirichlet plus Whitney-cup quadratic two- and three-minor energies plus mass potential on the normalized-affine admissible sector

final_topology.method: three regular values of the same normalized-affine map defined by the radially dressed third cup cochain

initial_topology.method: three regular values of the same normalized-affine map defined by the radially dressed third cup cochain

quotient_observables.quadrature: each exact tetrahedral action contribution lumped equally to its four vertices

theory.classical_action_identification_requires: a fixed-degree graph-norm density theorem or independent regularity of the continuum minimizer
theory.configuration: nodal S3 fields; P1 affine interpolation v_h; normalized-affine u_h=v_h/|v_h|; pairwise tetrahedral dot products greater than epsilon
theory.current_equiintegrability: the positive third-cochain Hodge term gives all third minors in L2; together with the affine-norm lower bound and strong finite-Lp convergence of v_h, the normalized-affine topological currents are uniformly integrable and converge weakly
theory.equicoercivity: the Dirichlet term bounds v_h in H1; nodal unit length and the interpolation estimate ||1-|v_h|||_L2 <= C a ||Dv_h||_L2 force every strong-L2 limit to be S3-valued
theory.full_unrelaxed_density_blocker: density of smooth fixed-degree maps in the exact finite-energy Skyrme graph norm is not proved here; unconditional Gamma convergence is therefore stated for the fixed-degree lower-semicontinuous relaxation
theory.liminf: convex lower semicontinuity in the complete minor vector (Dv, wedge^2 Dv, wedge^3 Dv) proves the compatible polyconvex liminf
theory.smooth_recovery: nodal interpolation of every smooth S3 map is eventually admissible; Whitney exactness gives convergence of the Dirichlet and Skyrme terms and preserves degree
theory.third_minor_identification: bounded nodal fields converge strongly in every finite Lp; Piola writes each third minor as div(v_h cof Dv_h)/3, so weak-L2 convergence of the second minors identifies the third minors distributionally
theory.two_minor_compensated_compactness: the Whitney two-cochain is exactly the de Rham cochain of dv_h^A wedge dv_h^B; its L2 bound and the distributional null-Lagrangian identity identify every weak limit with the two-minor of Dv
